I have nothing against Indigo and am perfectly happy to fly them for short domestic flights in India but anything longer than a hour and half with them makes for an uncomfortable flight.

Another great flight. Very consistent service, consistent quality of food and comfortable aircraft. The service on the flight was just spot on. I would recommend them based on how consistent they are.
Amazing from beginning to end. Amazing service, food and cabin. The variety and the quality of the food is mind blowing. The service was spot on and attentive. The seat was comfortable and private.
This flight was just the dream! 14hrs of first class service with top quality crew, amazing food, great Q Suite. The flight was so smooth, and pleasant, the hospitality was amazing.

Dammam with a stop in Doha en route to Boston they ruined a checked roller bag of mine. 3 months later, I have yet to get any resolution. They have delayed repeatedly, sending me in an endless circle of unresponsive bureaucracy. I have lost respect for what was once my favorite airline.

Checkin, security and immigration at DXB airport swift, lounge with good a la carte options and friendly service. Boarding well organized. Delayed by 45 minutes due to bad weather. Flight uneventful. Despite delayed arrival, remote stand with bus transfer. It took me 50 minutes from arrival at remote stand till arrival at the terminal. My plan to have a nice breakfast at the lounge was shattered. I had just a 10 minutes at the Platinum/Gold lounge, but left because there was no seat available, no clean cup to make a coffee, and long toilet queues. My flight to Bangkok was a remote stand bus gate. Boarding was poorly organized, as Business class and status guests were asked to wait till Economy has done boarding; the gate agent changed his mind and let C Class and status guests on the bus after 2 bus loads of Y class passengers had gone. It was rather chaotic. The flight on a leased CX aircraft was on time and uneventful. The IFE screens in the bulkhead rows of my section were broken, but large IPads were offered as a replacement, which was great. My food was mediocre. The flights were ok, but the chaotic transfer at Hamad airport left a bitter aftertaste.

First leg was in Economy, 2nd in First. Check in at BKK airport fast and friendly. Contract lounge at the new Satellite terminal large, but limited western breakfast options. Boarding started 30 minutes late and another 15 minutes wait at the air bridge. Service was good with slightly improved F&B offerings. I was lucky to be on an aircraft with their upgraded WIFI services which worked real well. 3-4-3 configuration with rather hard seats. Transfer in Doha was good with a stay at the Al Safwa F lounge. Food was good. My final leg to Dubai was changed from a Qsuite to a 2-2-2 configuration. After many back and forth, they were willing to change to another Qsuite flight 30 minutes later. The flight was pleasant and uneventful.

Qatar Airways changed the aircraft and did not notify us. We entered the Boeing 777 cabin to find that we had Paid for a Mercedes and would be flying in a Mini-Cooper. I didn't know airplanes with this drastically old configuration were still even in service. TIt was a miserable experience and we are changing our refundable return tickets to another airline. I won't be spending any money on Qatar Airways in the future!

On my way to Doha, it was amazing, flight attendants were very helpful and attentive. Doha to Colombo was delayed and after take off asked for a drink which I was told would be served in about 10 minutes, it took more than an hour for cabin crew to star serving drinks and food. After the service they never came back to ask if anything else was needed.

Both flights were excellent. Kind and professional staff on both flights, good food options (like that I can chose when to eat), comfortable flights. Due to distances at Doha airport, I appreciated that one of the small cart drivers offered his services to take me as close as possible to the train, connecting me to the center of the main terminal.

I have been a frequent traveller throughout my nearly 60 years as an air passenger. In all that time, I have never encountered such an unprecedented level of discourtesy as I did with the security staff at the Qatar Airways gate. at Doha. My husband and I had already smoothly passed through two transit security checks with Qatar Airways on our return journey from New Zealand. As we tried to board Business Class, I was surprised to be pulled over again for another bag check. The treatment I received was rude and demeaning. Everything in my rucksack was unpacked, which was then swabbed inside and out. I was manually body searched in front of everybody and my clothes, shoes and even hair was swabbed. This took place for a prolonged period of time in front of every other passenger that filed past - the flight had been called. When I subsequently touched one of the items in the second tray of my belongings (to open the zip to demonstrate there were only documents inside) the lady raised her voice, ordering me to stand back and not to handle any of my possessions. I tried to explain I was only trying to help but was met with more incivility. I would add that my husband, who was stood with me, had to witness this affront without any recourse to offer his assistance. When I was finally allowed to leave - the lady having found nothing of any suspicion - I felt utterly humiliated. This awful experience with Qatar Airways was only saved by the helpful and impeccable service of a wonderfully professional air steward on board called Eugenia - thank you kind lady. Her humanity and warmth restored my equilibrium. However the overall experience of our return journey was that, with the exception of Eugenia, the customer service is appalling. . I understand security is important but there was no need for my encounter to be as intimidating, embarrassing and uncivil as it was.

The food was really good, but the items listed on the menu, such as coconut cheesecake and stir-fried noodles, were unavailable. The seats were small but had amazing sleep amenities. The amenities kit was excellent. I requested an Asian vegetarian meal, which was very good, but it would have been better if there was naan or roti. The food quantity needs improvement. The legroom was good but could be better. This was my first time flying with Qatar Airways, and I would fly with them again.

So this was my first time on a 787-8. The seats are different on this plane compared to the Airbus A350. The 787-8 was really good, but I didn’t like the way the headrests were. The legroom was kinda tight with not much space, and I didn’t get a window seat—I got a middle row seat instead. But the flight was amazing. That mango juice is too good, omg! The food was great as well. There was palak paneer, but there was no paneer, only corn.

Doha to Copenhagen in Business Class on an A350-900. This aircraft featured the renowned Qsuite configuration - an epitome of comfort and privacy. Qatar Airways’ onboard service was nothing short of world-class, delivering excellence at every touchpoint. Both the hard and soft product were executed to perfection, setting a standard that is unmatched in commercial aviation.

Goa (GOX) to Doha in Business Class on a 787-8 The seating on this aircraft was of the older configuration, yet exceptionally comfortable. Service, as expected, was impeccable - flawless execution in line with Qatar Airways’ consistently high standards. Upon arrival in Doha, disembarkation was via stairs rather than an aerobridge. This is where Qatar Airways truly excels: the moment the first batch of Business Class passengers boarded the transfer bus, there was no unnecessary delay. Rather than waiting for it to fill up, the bus departed within minutes - a hallmark of true premium service.

review_title: "excited to fly again on an A380"
review_text: Very excited to fly again on an A380, my favorite aircraft. Check-in was nice and friendly, loved the lounge and staff there too! While the male crew were nice and looked happy, the female staff were mostly sour. Did not appreciate when the crew put my meal on my tray while I was at the bar. When I returned, the food was stone cold. I discussed that with the excellent purser. After that, the staff were sickly sweet. I decided to spend most of the time at the bar. Shame, but the next flight on Qatar was again excellent.

All that Glitters is not Gold On board their aircraft, Qatar Airways aim to present a spotless and glamorous experience. But sadly the mask is slipping badly. The 3-4-3 configuration on their 777 fleet has rightly been described as a crime against humanity. My real complaint with the company comes however behind the scenes. If you EVER have to deal with customer services they are obstructive and unhelpful. I paid over £1000 for an Economy Convenience multi-city ticket with some inbuilt ticket flexibility. I wanted, and indeed needed for medical reasons, a 2 hour stop in Doha. I have to administer medication which it is not possible to do on board. It could, indeed, endanger other pax to do it on the aircraft rather than in the airport. So I was pretty miffed when Qatar changed their schedules and cut my connection time down to 1h20 and then refused to listen to me about it. They COULD have agreed to put me on the flight taking off 5 minutes before my original booking to keep my 2 hour layover intact, which was the same price, but they have refused to do this. This isn’t the first time I have bumped up against their intransigence and arrogance and, to be frank, I don’t think I shall ever fly with Qatar again. I would strongly warn you to be very very careful about using them.
route: London Heathrow to Hong Kong via Doha

review_text: I recently had the opportunity to fly to Prague with Qatar Airways, and I would like to commend the exceptional service and attention I received throughout my journey. I would like to specifically acknowledge Uttam and Wan, who went above and beyond to assist me in retrieving my phone that had fallen between my seat after the flight had already landed. I had to wait for an engineer to come on board, but they were on the floor with various tools trying to reach my phone. I am extremely grateful for their efforts. This is a prime example of going the extra mile, and I hope they receive the recognition they deserve. Please continue to uphold your excellent service standards.

Had a pleasant experience with Qatar Airways. The sector from KUL to DOH was not perfect. The plane used was old and with a 2-2-2 business class configuration. Less privacy and lack of storage space. The crew made up to the deficit. Lobster meal was very good. The next sector from DOH to IAH was perfect. Business class passengers were given much priority. The in-flight supervisor came to introduce herself to each and every business class passenger. The cabin crew worked tirelessly to serve every request. Wonderful. Qsuites business class is simply amazing. I managed to catch 8 hours of sleep all together. The quality of the food served was top-notched. I tried purchasing the WiFi service but not successful though. Overall, very good quality of production and service. Only grudge is probably the price. It was much higher than any other airlines.
route: Kuala Lumpur to Houston via Doha

review_text: Cabin crew were attentive and approachable. Very clean cabin and toilet. Modern in flight entertainment, easy to use and quick response. I was impressed when the Cabin Crew In Charge or Purser came and introduced himself. I enjoyed travelling with their widebody aircraft although on a flight which is less than 5 hours.

I recently flew with Qatar Airways from Doha to Amman on QR404, dated January 26th, and I must say, it was an outstanding experience! The check-in process was seamless, and the staff were friendly and efficient. The aircraft was modern and clean, with comfortable seating and ample legroom. The inflight entertainment system was impressive, offering a vast selection of movies, TV shows, and music. The food was delicious, with a variety of options to suit different tastes. What truly impressed me, however, was the exceptional service provided by the cabin crew. They were attentive, courteous, and went above and beyond to ensure that all passengers had a comfortable and enjoyable flight. Overall, I would highly recommend Qatar Airways to anyone looking for a top-notch flying experience. Keep up the fantastic work!

I fly often with Qatar. Over 10 times per year, and the service is always so good. Very nice staff both at ground and at Cabin. I usually fly Economy and rarely get upgraded or I more often use points to upgrade my self. But still on long flights from Europe to Asia over Doha on Economy is a great experience.

My recent flight from Calicut to Hamad Airport on January 25th, 2025, was generally uneventful, but had several areas where I believe Qatar Airways could improve the passenger experience. Firstly, the check-in process at Calicut Airport was extremely slow, despite having completed online check-in beforehand. The cabin temperature remained uncomfortably warm throughout most of the flight. The quality of the in-flight meals, while tasty, was somewhat diminished by smaller portions. My breakfast croissant was completely flattened. The seats felt less spacious and harder than I'm accustomed to. Finally, I was disappointed by the limited selection of movies available on Oryx One.

review_title: "consistently ranks at or near the top"
review_text: Doha to Goa (Mopa Airport) in Business Class on Qatar Airways. This short flight, lasting just under three hours, featured Qatar Airways' non-Qsuite Business Class seats. Despite the absence of Qsuite, the experience was supremely comfortable, as is expected. The onboard service was warm and attentive, complemented by their signature on-demand meal offerings. The seamless blend of comfort, service, and sophistication once again highlights why Qatar Airways’ Business Class consistently ranks at or near the top.

Copenhagen to Doha on Qatar Airways in Business Class in Dreamliner 787-9. This aircraft features Qatar Airways’ standard non-Qsuite Business Class seating configuration. Despite the absence of the Qsuite, the seats are exceptionally comfortable, ensuring a luxurious experience. The inflight service was flawless, showcasing their hallmark on-demand meal service. I was particularly impressed by the noticeable upgrade in meal quality. The vegetarian option was thoughtfully prepared, with a refreshing lightness compared to the heavier, oilier meals of previous years. Overall, this was an outstandingly comfortable flight that lives up to Qatar Airways’ reputation for delivering the finest Business Class experience in the skies.

Qatar Airways, economy class, Doha to Paris CDG, B777, 09 September 2024. As always with Qatar Airways, crew are genuinely welcoming and helpful, both on the ground and onboard. I was able to access the Silver Lounge in Doha. Again, crew (mostly from Qatar / Middle East) very welcoming. Food and drinks (including wine) of good quality. Boarding swift. Crew always ready to help. Food, drinks, movies onboard all fine. Qatar Airways still has a miles-based Frequent flyer system (contrary to other airlines within One World Alliance that now have a money-based system) - and this is really appreciated. Last, but not least, i however prefer to fly Airbus (A330 A359 A380) rather than its competitor. Will choose Airbus aircraft within One World Alliance whenever possible, on any given route.

I have never felt the need to give Qatar a negative review before. I am a Silver member, and travel exclusively Qatar Airways, and have done for many years but I will start looking at alternatives. My complaints were purely to in respect to the food and the food service. On this occasion it was so bad on both legs of my flight it did really spoil the experience. The plane was lovely, entertainment options good and seating comfortable. I will start with the food service, as a silver member you get a hostess visiting you at the start of the flight and ask you what you would like to eat come meal service, then to be told when the service arrives that that choice has run out. I normally sit with my wife in the central section and I have never fully understood why the food service is not synchronised to start at exactly the same rows on both sides of the plane. I was in seat D and my wife in seat E, the hostess served my meal and moved on. I asked politely if my wife could have her meal and was informed that he was not doing the E seats only the D seat and she would have to wait till the other server arrived, who was about 15 rows forward and nowhere near our seats. I explained that we like to enjoy our wine and meal together at the same time, and that we were silver members and that someone had already taken our orders. He said he was allocated only to the D seats and could not do anything about it. I had finished my meal and had my tray cleared away and was served coffee, my wife still had not recived her meal, or a drink. It was some 45 minutes after I finished my meal that my wife required her meal and this was very disappointing and could have been resolved with flexibility by the staff. Now for the food Choices that consisted of rice with beef, or a chicken sausage wth frittata, and the 3rd which was pasta with a sauce.

review_title: "the flight with Qatar Airways was great"
review_text: Overall the flight with Qatar Airways itself was great, but the flight with one of its partner Malaysia Airlines to KUL is just terrible. For the Munich to Doha route, usually it is flown by the Boeing 787 or A350-900 which does not have QSuite, but on the day of my travel, we got the 777 with QSuite which was a huge upgrade from these types of planes that usually operate this route. Food was great, Staff was great, Entertainment was Good, WIFI is free for Privilege Club Member up to 1 hour. Arrived in Doha and we enjoy their Al Mourjan Business Lounge located at The Garden. Then we continue to Kuala Lumpur with Malaysia Airlines and the food was terrible, entertainment was very limited, WIFI was slow and staff was not so great. Qatar Airways needs to stop handing over their flights to their partners like MH and continue to operate their own daily flights instead.

We had a pretty disappointing flight. As we boarded in Barcelona, ​​I noticed that the flight attendants didn't seem particularly friendly. The plane was old, my partner's screen didn't work, and the seat in front of me was difficult to recline. Service on the night flight didn't start until 1.5 hours after takeoff, which pretty much ruined our night. The quality of the food has been better before, there were no refills of drinks and no coffee before landing let alone breakfast. We are very disappointed this time and hope that it was just a bad exception and that Qatar will maintain its level in 2025.

flew with Qatar Airways for a round trip between Paris CDG and Manila, with a layover in Doha. The overall experience was outstanding, both on the outbound and return journeys. The layovers were perfectly coordinated: around 2 hours on the outbound flight and less than an hour on the return. Despite their short duration, everything was handled smoothly and efficiently, without any stress. Onboard, the service was impeccable, with an attentive and professional crew. The food served was of high quality, and the seats were exceptionally comfortable, even on a long-haul flight. I highly recommend Qatar Airways for its seamless organization, exceptional service, and commitment to passenger comfort
route: Paris CDG to Manila via Doha

review_text: Qatar Airways is the absolute best airline ever! The service from the time of checking in to disembarkation is extremely thorough and professional. On board, the cabin crew is more than just polite, they are warm and friendly. They engage in conversation with you about your trip as if they are genuinely interested in you enjoying your journey and they are happy that they get to be a part of the process. I also liked that you never have to repeat a request. They listen carefully and follow through even with things like "Please wake me up 2 hours before we land" and try their best to accommodate special requests. The team on board this particular flight was one of the best I have experienced. They worked in unison to deliver guest satisfaction. It felt like they actually liked each other and were happy. I want to specially mention Fares who was catering to my side of the cabin. He had a warm, engaging smile. He was attentive and professional. I enjoyed his mannerism and I noticed that he took time to make every single person in his aisle feel special. To conclude, the crew for other airlines are polite, yes, but on Qatar, I feel they are more than just robotic smiling. They choose people who naturally understand good service and treat everyone with equal importance.

My flight was almost 4 hours late and I knew I was going to miss my transfer flight to Guangzhou. When I arrived to Doha I was really disappointed, that nothing was arranged for the missed flight. Instead many passengers were requested to wait in a hall for further arrangement. This was little bit weird, since the flight was delayed 3h and the flight itself took 7h. Why wasn't everything organized for the customers that you knew would miss their connecting flight? Two persons were assigned to help the customers, but that was one of the most confusing experiences I have ever had in an airport. Two guys running around like headless chickens, talking in a low voice and many people couldn’t hear what they say, stopping in middle of the way when some passer by stops them and ask for advice. I had to be alert all by myself, constantly checking when will my new connection flight can be arrangeme. It's either your system is lacking or those two guys are incompetent. One of the guys was also, not only incompetent, but arrogant. Telling customers that an accommadation is arranged in VIP-hotel, then a person ask what's the name of the hotel, he obnoxiously says he just stated the name of the hotel. Instead of clearing the confusion about the name of the hotel, he decides to belittle the tired and confused customer. When I finally got my connection flight ticket sorted and was ready to leave the airport he dragged me out the custom line after almost an hour waiting in the queue. Saying that they won't let out, because I have alcohol with me. He has to see some other arrangement for me. So I followed him, thinking that I wasn't allow to enter the city. In middle of the way he stops and starts to help other people, totally ignoring my existence. After helping others, he noticed me and said to me that my alcohol would be confiscated if I tried to get pass customs and he have to see if he could arrange for me to stay in a lounge room. Why didn't he fully tell me about my options first hand? I thought there were no options for me to leave the airport. It was almost morning. I haven't slept in almost 24hrs and he is suggesting me to stay in a lounge for rest of the time. There was over 20hrs until my next flight. I can’t stay in a lounge where there is no privacy. I refused his incompetent, useless help and wanted to get back to the customs. I demanded him to escort me there, because I didn’t want to queue again, but he refused and said he did his best to help me. Also I almost missed my next day connection flight, because the attendant told me I was arranged to 02.30 flight, but it was actually 1.30 flight. I had to run like crazy in the airport because I got there late and almost missed the flight.

review_title: "I was not recognized in any way"
review_text: As an emerald platinum member of Finnair Oneworld alliance, I was not recognized in any way: no welcome drink, no words of welcome. On the HKG-Doha flight, which took 9 hours 30 minutes, I was served was a disgusting snack and a mediocre breakfast with coffee that tasted burnt. The cabin temperature was terribly cold and nothing was done to change things. The icing on the cake was the distant, even contemptuous attitude of the flight attendants to me.
route: Hong Kong to Paris CDG via Doha

review_text: In my opinion the best airline in the world. From the moment we booked our tickets they contacted us several times to inform us even about secondary issues. The itinerary and stopover in Doha the best we could imagine. All 4 flights left right on time. The food was really good and delicious. The behaviour of the flight attendants was perfect, friendly and professional. Tickets prices were competitive. It was my first time flying with this company and it will be a real pleasure to fly with them again

Athens to Doha was good service but Doha to Tokyo was poor, attendant service very slow (Ath~Doh had 4 people serving economy meal at the same time, but Doha - Tokyo had 2 people serving my economy area). My meal quality was poor and amount of drinks as required. (Ath~Doh) offered drinks after meals and once during 3 hours flight and after meal, but Doha Tokyo offered drinks during the meals but not after meals, on request. Good seat quality and space.

Exceptional service, always kind and did their best to make you feel comfortable. Did not have a q suite and the seats may need be refreshed but did not matter when you get nice service good food drinks . I never felt discriminated as i often do because i am overweight, it may sound stupid but for me who travels a bit, that feeling is horrible. Thanks Qatar. Keep up the good job!!!
As always superb experience! Perfect service, friendly and attentive cabin crew, clean and fresh cabin. Food excellent!

As always superb experience! Perfect service, friendly and attentive cabin crew, clean and fresh cabin. Food excellent! And took me back to Air France in the 90s on long-haul flights, a lounge in the business class cabin. Excellent for stretching your legs, get a drink and mingle with other passengers. Splendid idea! But not free basic WiFi.

I have flown them 6 times in the last year from Madrid to South Africa via Doha and in business class. Those who have flown on their Q-Suites (777), 787s, A350s, and their older 777 where it was 2 seats on the window with no real divider or privacy, understand there is a huge difference in quality between their planes and the experience overall. When choosing my route for an upcoming trip with them I chose a route that had a Q-suite followed by a 787, as they offer the most privacy and comfort. I just received notice that they have downgraded my plane from Doha to Johannesburg to their oldest configuration of their 777 where there are 2 seats on the window, no real divider, and no privacy at all. I understand that sometimes changes are necessary, however I've spent 3 days now trying to get them to change the 1 leg of my journey, the leg where they downgraded the plane to the old 777, to an alternative flight that would simply put me back on the 787. I'm not looking for an upgrade to a Q-suite, I just don't want to be on the same plane I booked and paid for. However, their service team refuses to make the change without me paying a fare difference. Apparently the only time they will allow a free change is if they downgrade you from the Q-suite, which is ridiculous. If they moved it from a 787 to an A350 it would have been fine, however their old 777 configuration is by no means anywhere near the same quality of experience. There is no flex, no desire to keep your business. I realize I am complaining about a business class experience, which may seem petty to some, however when you fly a lot, spend a lot of money to do so, there should be an expectation of service from the partners you choose.

There was serious confusion conveyed by flight attendants to passengers after arriving in Phuket. “Passengers flying on to Penang should disembark at Phuket.” “Passengers going on to Penang should stay seated.” “Passengers who will go on to Penang should come forward now to disembark.” “You should not be trying to disembark yet.” Now you need to disembark at the rear of the plane.” “You should not be coming back here. Return to the front.” Then a scheduled 90 minute layover became 3 hours. Upon arrival in Penang Qatar did not get a jetway, and it was raining hard. After moving forward to disembark we were told by the pilot that the airport is requiring us to sit on the aircraft for 30 minutes until the rain stops. About 40 minutes later the pilot informed us that we will have to wait at least another 10 minutes.

The food and choices on the long haul from Bos-Doh was quite stressful. The Economy regular meals were to die for to be honest- some of the best chicken biryani, which you wouldn’t see in the special meal menus. This time it was stuff that neither me nor my family could get half way through. Either too bland or salty. Also the service was slow - kids get super cranky on late night flights without food and no butter, salt, pepper and sugar. Unlike many other airlines, QR’s economy class is really good. In fact that was my biggest selling point for wanting to fly QR this time- collecting air miles is not an easy job! An older aircraft having >90% occupancy with an out of order toilet plus wi-fi doesn’t help. On the positive side, massive shout out to QR ground staff from Bos for a smooth path from check in to boarding. Also to the Doh- MAA flight pursers for explaining the shortage of on board meal options- only chicken was available in the general category.

It started when we checked in at Maldives and the check-in couldn't give us any boarding passes for the second flight between Doha and Copenhagen. They asked us to contact the Qatar Airways service desk after arriving in Doha. We did this upon arrival but was then referred to another Qatar Airways service desk. After queuing our way through the third service desk, we were told that they could not give us a boarding pass due to the flight being overbooked. They wanted us to go to some other service desk several times. After 3 hours of waiting we finally got boarding shortly after check-in at the gate opened. We had to rush to the gate. Since I had not received a boarding pass at the airport, I could not do any purchases from the airport shops as I normally do. While I wasn't checked in, all the updates via Qatar Airways' app on my mobile didn't show up, which features I appreciate. I have sent an email to their customer service support but no feedback in a month.

Qatar Airways is using Oman Air planes and crew on certain sectors. This sector was changed a few months after I booked my ticket, and I didn’t mind so much because I was keen to experience Oman Air. The business class layout is ok, and the seat was very comfortable. There were only 3 passengers travelling in business class on this flight and we were well attended to. The food and wine was good. Nothing more, nothing less. The crew were very pleasant and enjoyed a chat. I find the Qatar entertainment very limited and not a wide selection of movies. The flight itself was very smooth. Overall, a comfortable 5 hours in the air.

Outstanding flight and crew. The food and wine were superb. The FA looking after our section was outstanding in her quiet delivery of her service. I had a very different experience on each of the four sectors I flew, and this sector together with the Doha/Nairobi sector were on par. Attentive service, good food and a comfy seat. The entertainment choices are very limited so I usually end up listening to my own music. The seat was very comfortable and I managed to get 6 hours sleep.

I recently flew Qatar Airways in business class, expecting the renowned QSuite experience. However, the aircraft was changed, and the QSuite was not available. The issues didn’t end there. When I contacted customer service to inquire about rebooking, the agent I spoke with was unhelpful and uninterested in assisting. Unfortunately, the service on board was equally disappointing. During boarding, the cabin crew paid little attention to passengers, creating a lack of warmth and hospitality. The in-flight dining service was far below the standard I expected: The dine-on-demand service was not offered. When I requested mezze, I was told it was unavailable. My alternative request for miso resulted in receiving a completely different dish. For the main course, two out of four options on the menu were unavailable. I was served chicken that appeared to be from the economy menu, and it was tasteless. My second meal, sushi, was barely edible. Even basic items like orange juice for a mocktail were not available. For a 12-hour flight, the limited meal selection and poor quality were unacceptable. When I raised my concerns with the purser, I did not receive an apology but was told I should have pre-ordered my meal—despite not being informed of this requirement beforehand. This experience was far from the premium service Qatar Airways promises its business class passengers. I hope the airline addresses these shortcomings to ensure better service in the future.

"Love at First Flight". I took Qatar Airways for the first time two years ago. As a frequent business traveler, customer experience, value for money and safety are the two most important factors. In my opinion, Qatar Airways achieved full marks in all the three areas. Qatar Airways is now my No. 1 choice. Some of the meals that you have provided onboard have been one of the best experience that I have had over thirty years of flying. I feel treated as a "First Class" passenger on Economy class.

The flight lived up to expectations, though this older aircraft did not have as wide a range of movies loaded into its IFE compared to the flights operated by newer A350 and 787. Boarding of the aircraft was efficient and respected the assigned seating Zones. Good service from cabin crew, who were very courteous. The first meal service was a bland chicken sandwich (because the flight operated late at night, when most people just wanted to sleep). A full cooked meal service was provided later in the flight for breakfast, however. The flight operated on time.

Chaotic check-in at Kathmandu. The airport struggles to cope with volume of passengers, both land-side and air-side. The flight departed an hour late due to local air traffice restrictions, despite the incoming aircraft having arrived on time. The aircraft was one of the older ones in the fleet, and the IFE was not touch screen, nor did it have the same breadth of range of entertainment options as offered on Qatar's newer aircraft. However, service was good, including food & beverage offerings, despite the aircraft being fully occupied. Cabin crew worked hard and were always polite and attentive. As a result of the late departure, we arrived in Doha an hour late, too. The aircraft was parked a long bus ride away from the terminal, which didn't help the delay.

The best thing about this airline is the good training of its staff. They have been trained to be polite even in the face of problems. It goes a long way to making the experience more pleasant than it otherwise could have been. The booking experience via their website was not fully satisfactory as the seat map could not be accessed at the time of booking nor via 'Manage your booking' after the booking was made. I had to ring up the call centre to request them to book my specific seats. The call-centre staff were polite and very willing to help, but the quality of the phone line was poor. Online check-in could not be fully completed, requiring manual document checks at the airport (Heathrow) for the issue of the boarding pass. However, the queues for the self-service check-in machines at the airport was very long and every passenger seemed to require manual documentation check by staff (who were too few to cope with this - but handled it politely)s. Onboard service was excellent and plenty of food provided for the flight. IFE had an impressive selection of movies and TV shows.

Crew were hardworking and attentive on this full flight. Good provision of food. Excellent range of inflight entertainment. Flight took off and landed on time. My only gripe is that it was difficult to reserve my seat in advance- the online seat map was not working on their website nor on their app, and I had to ring up the call centre to sort it out.

review_title: "leave the queue to adjust the weight"
review_text: Traveling from Delhi to Düsseldorf, we encountered a long check-in queue, some open counters in the economy class were left unattended, with staff chatting instead of assisting passengers. We had an allowed baggage weight of 100 kg for our four tickets, each with a 25 kg limit. We checked in two bags, but the staff said that one bag weighed 27 kg instead of 25 kg and demanded payment for the extra 2 kg. We had to leave the queue to adjust the weight and upon returning, were asked to start from the end of the line. Onboard I had to settle for an Indian vegetarian meal, which I did not enjoy. While Doha airport was beautiful, we were transported to our connecting flight to Düsseldorf by bus, which was uncomfortable.
The staff were not friendly to me at check in. The seats aren't comfortable and my food did not taste good. Planes are smaller than some airlines.

I travelled with a baby on Qatar Airways. Though I added the baby late, they auto-assigned me a seat with a bassinet. On the flight, they were so kind and helpful. They had baby food and diapers. They helped me fetch milk from my bag. When the baby poured food all over me, they helped me clean up, including a member who wiped my feet. They were so kind and helpful - I was very moved.

The plane was fine, for a long flight it is important, but the bathrooms were dirty. The meals they offer, you can choose if you are in the front, from the middle to the back, they only offer you what is left, pasta. The same at breakfast, if you don't want the option they give you, they tell you to wait until they serve the entire plane. You can ignore all this, but when you arrive at your destination and they have lost your luggage, the disappointment is total. And worse, when upon returning home, they lose your luggage again, they deliver it to you later, the suitcases are broken, they follow the instructions of their call center for the claim, and finally they do not take responsibility for the damages.
route: Ho Chi Minh City to Barcelona via Doha

review_text: I took QR701 from Doha to JFK on Nov 11. I was flying business class and was seated in 10A. After we took off, the refreshing towel that was given before mwas still on my counter for about 30 minutes. I was served with breakfast 30 minutes after we took off. They left me for the next 8 hours on a 13 and 1/2 hours flight. I was taking naps off and on but lost track of time. No one checked if I needed anything. When I requested to have my meal served, it took about an hour and half from start to finish. The service was slow. No one was being served at that time around me. My dessert plate sat on my table for another 30 minutes after I finished. I asked for a drink about 2 hours to landing, no one picked up after for an hour or so.

I have unfortunately witnessed a decline in service quality and value for money. My recent experience on a flight from JFK to Doha was particularly disappointing. The onboard meal service was far below expectations. I had ordered a special Asian vegetarian meal, but the food was of poor quality—extremely salty dal, undercooked rice, and watery, tasteless paneer left much to be desired. The inflight entertainment options were equally disappointing, with no new films available. Instead, the selection consisted of outdated blockbusters, making it hard to pass the time on a long-haul flight. While the new Boeing 777 aircraft offered moderately comfortable seats, the overhead storage was poorly designed, causing significant inconvenience. Adding to the frustration was the baggage policy enforcement. Qatar Airways enforced a single carry-on policy, which I assume is due to the overhead bin space. This policy feels overly restrictive and diminishes the overall travel experience. The service from the cabin crew was another letdown. What once set this airline apart—exceptional service, quality meals, and attention to detail—now feels like a distant memory.
route: New York to Mumbai via Doha

review_title: “cut my trip short by one day"
review_text: My initial flight (QR 649) was scheduled on November 12 at 9:30 am from Kathmandu to Doha. On November 7, Qatar canceled my flight because of "operational issues." I was told that Nepal's airport renovation had resulted in several airline cancellations over the previous eight months. Qatar was aware of this problem for a long time because I made my travel arrangements in April 2024. My original flight (QR 649) was changed from 9 am to 7 pm on November 12 and there were other Qatar flights from Kathmandu to Doha on November 12 however Qatar never accommodated me to take one of those flights. Rather, I was instructed to fly from Kathmandu to Doha on November 18 or from New Delhi to Doha on November 11. I chose to fly from Delhi on November 11 because I had to return to Chicago by November 13 for work. Additionally, Qatar informed me that I would need to arrange my own airfare from Kathmandu to Delhi. I had to pay $500 to another airline for a ticket from Kathmandu to Delhi. I was informed by Qatar that I would not receive reimbursement for this flight. To get home to Chicago, I had to pay an additional $500 and cut my trip short by one day. Furthermore, Qatar misled me by claiming that there would be no additional flights on November 12.

Flew to London last week and the journey was totally flawless. On the return journey wanted to make a date change, so went to the terminal 4 hoping that this would be quicker and simpler. The ticketing staff was unhelpful and I had to stick to the original itinerary. Upon returning to Doha today early morning, there was NO jet bridge so passengers had to uae the stairs.

Disappointed with the service on the Melb to Doha leg. The FA was reluctant to serve me champagne before take off and when I asked for another glass during dinner, conveniently forgot. I had to ask for my tray to be clreaed after dinner. The same crew member made up the bed which again had to ask. The main meal was ordinary, not the same standard as in the past. The Qsuite was nice and comfortable and managed to sleep 8 hours. I would rate this flight 3 out of 5. My flight from Doha to Nairobi was outstanding. The FA were magnificent, efficient, served me champagne even though it was 2am without any question or judgement. The meals were nice. The flight in the A350 . I paid BC as this is my trip to celebrate my 70th and was disappointed in the standard of service in the first part of the journey but definitely not the second.

I always fly business class with Qatar Airways and this is the first time I got disappointed with the service, especially with the food. They serve me cold omelette and I asked for cappuccino they serve me like a glass of milk with a small amount of coffee.

I booked business class KTM-DOH-LHR and back with British Airways and Qatar Airways. 2 days before my flight back from London I get a message stating they have cancelled my connecting flight from Doha to KTM and replaced it with a flight next morning, with a 10 hour layover. I expect them to do is provide transit accommodation in Doha, when they they chose to put me through the unwanted 10 hour layover but they don't. I am asked to land in Doha and try my luck with the transfer desk, I am not amused by the thought of running around for accommodation past midnight when my flight lands. I call them, chat with them, my agent in KTM files a request , my travel agent sends a mail, and I filed a formal complaint. Nothing.

Worst experience in over 50 years. 2 days before they cancel the flight for operational reasons and offer no alternative route despite having a flight a day before which I was willing to take and assume the cost of the extra night stay. I have traveled with them before, ok in flight, but will never expose myself to this lack of service again.

I paid a premium price and did not receive a premium experience. The aircraft on both legs was very dated, WIFI did not work and the entertainment was terrible. On both flights my main course was very dry and tasteless. My flight between Durban and Doha was chaotic, the cabin was half empty and I still had to keep asking for water or to have a second glass of wine. Drink quality was good but very controlled.

This was my first time flying Qatar as I feel that Emirates are now getting very dated especially on the Dublin/ Dubai route. Qatar was excellent between Dublin and Doha. Cabin was modern, service was excellent and food was the best I have had on a flight . I really enjoyed it. The flight between Doha and Cape Town was not so good. It was a much older cabin but as it was a late night flight I did not want food so I cannot really comment on service and food quality although I was disappointed with the seat and the entertainment. There was very little selection, and some of the TV series were season 2 or season 3 which does not make sense if season one is not available. This part of my trip really did bust my bubble. Wifi on both legs was terrible and really not worth the effort.
route: Dublin to Cape Town via Doha

review_text: My check in process through the Business counter was excessively long. The staff member had no idea what he was doing. The Economy counter next to me pumped out 3-4 people while this man pondered my check in at snail pace. It was very frustrating, but only the beginning of what was to come. On boarding the aircraft I realised it was a small A320 and had I known that at the time of the upgrade offer, I would never have paid for it. The seat is nothing more than a premium economy, it barely reclined, there was no footrest, there was no entertainment unit (unless you count an annoying stand alone IPad). Honestly, it was horrible. The seat was not comfortable at all, the aircraft was freezing, the windows were absolutely filthy with caked on muck BUT the icing on the cake was inflight service. It was very ordinary and although the food menu presented well and I enjoyed my appetizer and main, they forgot my dessert and coffee altogether. What a waste of money.

Excellent Service by the Flight Attendants on Qatar Airways (Flight QR 976, October 20). I recently flew with Qatar Airways on Flight QR 976 from Doha to Hanoi on October 20, in Business Class, and had the pleasure of being assisted by two amazing flight attendants, Yu Hee and Anastasia. Their service was outstanding from start to finish. They were attentive, friendly, and professional, ensuring that all passengers in Business Class were comfortable and well taken care of. Yu Hee and Anastasia’s warm attitude and attention to detail truly stood out, and they went above and beyond to make the flight a pleasant experience. Whether it was answering questions or offering assistance, they did it all with a smile and genuine care. I want to extend my sincere thanks to these wonderful flight attendants for their excellent service. They truly made a difference in my travel experience, and I look forward to flying with Qatar Airways again.

I've had the pleasure of flying with various airlines over the years, but Qatar Airways remains my top choice. Their exceptional service consistently impresses me, from lounge amenities to seamless departures and in-flight experiences, culminating in stress-free arrivals. Qatar Airways has earned my loyalty, and I wholeheartedly recommend them to friends and colleagues.

Qatar airways have been excellent in all aspects, starting from booking / changing you tickets online, promptness in flight timings, professionalism and courtesy of crew members and ground floor. Their only aim is to make you happy. Aircraft is always very clean, comfortable seating, great variety of entertainment, excellent quality of headphones and screens, food in first class could be easily Michelin star rated, great experience and I always look forward to travel in QA.

Booked with Qatar last minute, glad I did. I downloaded the app which made changing dates and seats a breeze, and it has 247 live chat which is brilliant. First leg on new 777-300ER layout. I found the seats not very comfortable (23C exit seat). Entertainment was fantastic, more than enough to watch, almost like having Netflix onboard. My screen had issues which was frustrating though. Staff very friendlya and approachable. Food very good! Second leg not as good. Same plane but older layout. Food not as good. Really horrible sandwich, and my breakfast was only OK, not that appetising. Seat also not very comfortable (35C exit seat). I always have to pay for exit seats because of my height. I get annoyed when people see empty ones and sit in them when they haven't paid. There were quite a few empty seats on both legs of the journey, yet when I booked 48hrs before departure, there was only 1 seat left. Toilets kept quite clean, but the bins were overflowing on both flights. Both flights delayed without explanation. Flight arrived 10mins early at final destination. Doha airport I find good. My second flight was not shown on the screens though, so there were a lot of people trying to flag down staff to check what gate.

Evening departure from BKK. Qatar really struggle with boarding on occasions and this was no different. Seats were changed at the gate as one of our reserved one’s were wet. Allocated into the rear cabin 38A + B. Seemed to be an older aircraft with an older configuration in the business cabin. Busy flight. The seat proved to be very comfortable and superbly thought out. The best there is I think. Safety issue of individuals roaming around the cabin aisles looking for empty seat rows, including someone moving up and down the aisle with his bags, opening and shutting overhead bins blocking the aisle While the aircraft is taxiing under power for departure. Something that should of been jumped on very quickly, but wasn’t. Food was exceptional for Economy, as was the service from a hard working pleasant crew. Overall this sector proved that this is the best Medium to long haul Economy cabin product there is. . Doha Hamad Airport is exceptional and a pleasant transfer. Second Sector on a Boeing 787-8. 19F + G either side of an aisle. Flight appeared to be full and Departed on time, early hours of the morning. The seat’s on this Aircraft were significantly less comfortable. A . 3-3-3 configuration and the ACDFGJ seats each have an under seat IFE box mounted on it’s side that restricts the width of legroom available. Seats appear far narrower, and not so well thought out. Although I find the cabin appealing and the ride pleasant. I’ll generally avoid this particular type of Aircraft. because I find them particularly uncomfortable in Economy, especially on a busy flight.. Again, exceptional food, great Service and a lovely hard working crew. Nonetheless, overall a very nice Economy experience, highly recommend it.

As a frequent flyer with Qatar Airways, I can confidently say that their service is consistently exceptional. The hospitality, from check-in to arrival, is outstanding; staff are courteous, attentive, and always ready to assist. Their scheduling is reliable and well-organized, making flights punctual and connections smooth. The ease of booking, online or through the app is incredibly user-friendly, allowing me to arrange trips without hassle. The lounge facilities are superb, offering a quiet, luxurious space to relax before flights with excellent dining options. Both ground and inflight services are top-notch, with a focus on comfort, quality, and efficiency. Qatar Airways truly sets a high standard in global air travel!

Doha to Athens plane was small with box that blocked leg room window seats. As a person with a disability I could not move, request to move was met with only had middle seat, as business class is only for people that pay for them. When I requested to pay by points for upgrade was told none available. When people in front chose to recline hostess reaction they are allowed to recline however when we tried our seats were rudely pushed upright by passengers. I have emailed Qatar as yet no response.

In the past 10 days or so I've taken 4 long haul business class flights with Qatar and on all of them it's been like being in a 5 star hotel / restaurant. The cabin crew treated me like a VIP, the food & drinks were excellent and the facilities (seat, entertainment, wifi, toilets etc) were all fantastic. It is a real pleasure flying QA. The same goes for the lounges I used in Doha with the only negative being the lounges which they don't control outside of Doha.

Absolutely terrible trip. Started off well from Dubai to Doha nice 777 in first class which is business class rebranded for this route. Once in Doha the fun started in the Business Class lounge I was told I could only stay 30 minutes and access to the restaurant area was denied because they were busy, so no hot food. Staff were robotic and not at all interested. The connecting flight was an old A320 with a business class seat which was broken and they said cannot be fixed. Hardly any legroom and cramped. Poor inflight entertainment selection and no wifi. Breakfast portions dismal, left the flight hungry.

I discovered that my luggage had been severely damaged during the journey. The crack spanned across the depth of the luggage, from the zipper to the wheel area, and the inner linings of my baggage were clearly visible. As I had left the Airport premises when the damage was found and I acknowledged that there might not be anything the airline could have done for me, their response was the most unhelpful I have ever received. My return flight on 29 September was cancelled. The instructions to collect our baggage and hotel vouchers were poorly communicated, we only heard muffled announcements and did not receive any email or text message on what to do next. It was chaotic and the airline staff were unhelpful, it took us more than 4 hours just to collect our baggage and vouchers at the airport, before we boarded the bus to the assigned hotel. Following my trip, I also reached out to Qatar Airways Media/Customer Service to share my feedback, however, it does not seem like they took my feedback seriously. They took more than 10 days to inform me that they had forwarded my feedback on baggage irregularities to the Baggage department in Zurich.

The experience from boarding, which was chaotic and confusing, to the seat, entertainment, staff and food was abysmal. Qatar have really gone downhill. During boarding the staff were giving contradictory messages to the passengers, with one person saying a certain group could board and another saying they couldn't. The economy seats are hard, small and uncomfortable. I was able to book an economy comfort++ for one leg of my journey. It should have been two but they changed the hardware, so I was bumped to economy. These seats are much better but it's a lottery if you can book one. My food servings were tiny, and a couple of the meals I couldn't finish they were that bad. The staff were didn't do anything above the bare minimum. The entertainment system on some planes is outdated and unresponsive, although much better on the newer planes. However, the selection of films is quite poor.

I have flown with QR 8 times this month, economy and business class. The airline is pretty good especially in economy as a Privilege club member with status, expedited check in and lounge access for both Silver & Gold levels. On board assuming you pick the right seat it's comfortable The food is good as is the service, it's very good and probably one of the best. Business class is great on any aircraft, I had my first experience of Q Suites which was nice, although I like the A380 too with a bit more space and a lounge to use. My 8th flight this month in Q suites from DOH to LHR was probably the least enjoyable, boarding was a shambles, the aircraft was a 20 minute bus ride away and once on board remained at 30c + for 45 minutes, the crew were just okay. Food options not available and the area around my seat is not cleaned with rubbish left by the previous passenger. Clearly QR and DOH airport offer great options, we all have off days so hopefully this is what happened with my last flight. Otherwise I will continue to use QR on my 8 to 10 trips each year, they really are customer focused and offer a great product they just need to get there boarding process sorted it's a bit of a let down sometimes.

Rude Staff Upon arrival at the airport, we encountered dismissive behavior from a Qatar Airways supervisor, our concerns were met with a lack of empathy, which No Entertainment during our 7-hour flight, my wife and I were the only passengers without a functioning entertainment system. While I understand that technical issues can occur, there was no effort to provide alternatives. This left us frustrated and disappointed. I have reached out to Qatar Airways multiple times regarding these issues, but their responses have been generic and focused on unrelated matters, such as visa documentation. It feels as if our legitimate complaints are being overlooked. We chose Qatar Airways expecting world-class service, but we received anything but that. The lack of resolution has overshadowed what should have been a joyful occasion.
Flight ok to Doha but 6 hour delay on return with no compensation despite it not being due to weather etc. Poor customer service. Meals repetitive but crew were reasonable.
Wonderful experience, awesome staff ... very friendly and attentive, good food, clean cabin and toilets. Streaks ahead of other airlines. Will definitely be flying Qatar wherever I can.

review_title: "pleased with the overall comfort and service"
review_text: No hot towels are provided, and the sweet to suck to prevent your ears-popping are no longer given out and meals were notably smaller although still sufficient. We did online check-in and there was dedicated web bag-drop which was quick, and stress-free where the friendly ground staff issued us paper boarding passes. Boarding was scheduled early but on time, and the cabin crew on our first flight from London Gatwick to Doha seemed motivated, hospitable and keen to please providing service with a warm smile. The Boeing 787 on this route is very comfortable to travel on and we arrived early into Doha, where thankfully we could have a leisurely transfer via an airbridge to the terminal and even have some time to experience The Orchard at Doha Hamad as well as a quick spot of Duty Free shopping. Our second flight to Salalah was on time, staff were a little less polished but efficient and friendly. Meals including snacks on both sectors was excellent in quality - very tasty. I was pleased with the overall comfort and service, and it provided a relaxing start to our holiday in Oman.

Our outbound journey with Qatar Airways excellent and I was pleased standard had been maintained but our return journey showed how inconsistent they cab ne and that an excellent experience cannot always be counted on. After checking in online where it was communicated our flight from Doha to London would be on an A380 which we were pleased about, when we went to the airport to bag-drop we enquired if we could be seated on the upper deck economy cabin - the check-in assistant at Salalah airport was struggling to do this so consulted the Qatar Airways station manager there and he informed us the reason it wasn't possible was the plane had been changed to a 777. This plane is is cramped with 10 abreast rows when the plane is designed for 9 abreast, the seats have virtually no padding. On an overnight flight it was a very uncomfortable. The transfer experience early morning at Doha to get to that plane is stressful and chaotic - especially if they make you land at a remote stand, you will struggle to make your connecting flight. It is ridiculous to be using a remote stands and bussing people to the terminal via slow convoluted route at what is very new and modern airport. There is also bag security checks, once a general one then another random one at the gate for our flight back to London. My food was appalling on all sectors with this journey - the funny thing is on our Salalah to Doha sector we got the same meal (chicken mashkool) as on our way to Salalah a week ago, but whilst then it tasted amazing this time it tasted terrible. Cabin crew on both journeys were at best indifferent, but looked unhelpful, unmotivated, lacking warmth and miserable on the second flight from Doha to London Heathrow - this is meant to be one of their flagship routes, yet this is the standard of service.

Helsinki-Doha operated by Finnair. Excellent service and food very good for economy class, FAs almost perfect. Qatar Airways from Doha to Johannesburg. This flight was the biggest disappointment. Service was we don't care, we just work here. My food was disgusting. Drinks served in glasses.

review_title: "no cordoned off section for Business Class"
review_text: On 7 October 2024 we had waited for hours in Barcelona airport and were looking forward to relax in a pleasant Business Class lounge. Our dep 2250 for Doha flight checkin time was stated to be 7.45 pm, but the staff were busy chatting, not heeding the time and long queue for Economy. There was no cordoned off section for Business Class passengers. They finally opened 3 counters past check in time and it was a free for all with Economy class passengers shoving ahead. The Barcelona business class lounge, it was very disappointing. Most toilets did not work, there was hair and rubbish stuck in the sinks. In the lounge none of the power points worked for us to charge up our phones. The food offered was the worst in a Business Class Lounge. A few squares of cold lasagne, lots of snacks, and cleaning staff wheeled their wagons around the tables chucking in dirty tableware noisily. The flight to Barcelona to Doha took off late. As a result, in Doha we were not able to enjoy the Business Class lounge, and barely managed to get to our connecting flight to Kuala Lumpur, rushing through the crowded airport.
route: Barcelona to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

review_text: The flight from Rome to Doha was turned back and had to land in Turkey where the passengers stayed on board the plane for more than five hours before being sent to sit in seats at a departure gate. Without clear advice on the flight out of Turkey we eventually were placed on a flight to Doha. There was chaos at Doha airport with little help and communication in regard to flights to get home to Perth. The systems seemed inadequate to provide information to staff who were extremely inefficient in handling our flights back to Perth. After a further period of about 10 hours we were advised last minute of flights we were on. We had paid extra for the upper deck of an A380 plane from Doha to Perth and were placed in normal economy on the flight to Singapore. We were then placed on a Jetstar budget airline flight from Singapore to Perth. We arrived at our destination two days late after experiencing about 4 hours of sleep over the two day period. After seeking compensation for the Doha to Perth flight, particularly for the upper deck seats we never got, they advised me that no compensation will be paid.

This was my changed flight after I missed connection. All was well. Bit hot at the beginning as we were boarding on a tarmac from the bus. Flight was smooth. There was only one little mistake. Before service a male staff member asked for my food preference. I ordered beef. During service I got chicken and as the staff ignored my bell for a long period of time I walked to him and changed the meal. Otherwise I had great time and really enjoyed the flight. Thank you so much.

review_title: “happy with the way this trip was handled”
review_text: Very nice to be back onboard the mighty A380. Unfortunately due to the closure of the airport because of the storm we ended up delayed almost 2 hours. However as I understand that it was beyond Qatar crew control we were taken care of well. Watching movies and having a glass of drink while waiting for the ATC to finally allowed us to push. As a result of that unfortunately me and most of customers missed their connecting flights. One thing I would complain about was the fact that we were not able to find connection information on our screens so we had to wait until we got to the transfer desk. When on transfer desk I would strongly recommend to add more staff for economy section. I went to Silver/Platinum/Gold line and it was great but looking at the economy section it seemed very understaffed. Overall I was happy with the way how this whole trip was handled. I was booked on next available flight in 8 hrs and got to my destination rested and ready to go.

Amazed by the service on ground, in the air, quick baggage delivery, on time flights, clean aircraft, toilets, super entertainment. The air crew were particularly helpful and went out of their way to help, respectful and very professional. First time flew Qatar Airways but will keep an eye out when selecting airline in future. Keep it up!!

I will not be traveling with this airline again. They have worst food, worst luggage policy and worst customer service. Food was flavorless, and bland. During my recent travel, they served mushroom omelette, it was one of worst thing I tasted. They have changed their luggage policy for international travel to/from US to only allow one checked bag and one carry on (no more personal items such as backpack or purse). All this measures to make sure that they make maximum money from price gouging unsuspecting travelers. This creates unwinding lines at the airport because almost all the passengers have to shuffle their luggage or pay extra fees for their luggage.

review_title: "only get an aisle seat at the airport"
review_text: I selected & paid for the specific seat prior to the flight, not to bother with the remaining available seats at the airport. In the day of the travel I realised that the purchased seat in the middle area of the plane & aisle seat was changed to a middle seat. When I started the discussion via chat, they informed that the plane changed which is understood, but it doesn’t mean that the re-allocation shouldn’t consider what I purchased initially. After several discussions via live chat with no result, I only get an aisle seat at the airport (with additional discussions) from the remaining available seats. The persons travelling with me, without selecting a seat in advance received a better seat position. I raised a complaint and the customer service team returned with an email that they wouldn’t return the money/miles paid as I also received an aisle seat at the airport although not in the middle area of the plane. So the inconvenience created and the time spent with the discussions & having the seat allocated at the airport which is cost free while I paid for my seat - doesn’t matter.

review_title: "app is an utterly appalling user experience"
review_text: Return trip LHR via Doha to Kuala Lumpur via, economy. Check-in via laptop failed because log-in all day said "There's been an error. Please try later". The app failed to work because required fields (Nationality, and Country Code - already in my profile details, but not propagated to my booking) were inert and couldn't be completed - and so I couldn't proceed to the next page. Eventually, via laptop I got a Confirmation of Check-in, but no boarding passes - which meant queueing and check in at Heathrow anyway on the day of departure. A 1-hour connection time in Doha was reduced to 30 minutes due to late arrival of our first Qatar flight, and we had to cover a long distance to the next gate (including wife with 6-month old knee replacement). It was tense, and if they hadn't delayed the subsequent flight by 20 minutes we would never have made it. Return check-in the app didn't work. We had therefore to check in at the airport in Kuala Lumpur on the morning of the flight - along with dozens and dozens of other passengers presumably with the same issue. There was a massive queue as a result. It moved at a snail's pace because even with five desks open, the process and staff seemed laborious and slow. We barely made the gate in time, but had allowed two and a half hours for this. During the second return leg (Doha - London) the crew were pretty clumsy, one girl ramming the drinks trolley three times into my seat and the seat in front. I'm not oversized, but got barged by cabin crew going past twice, which is profoundly annoying when you're trying to sleep. The crew on this leg were not particularly friendly, although on other legs they were pleasant. The app is very good at suggesting you upgrade your seat or baggage (at cost) but is so short of real-time data as to be useless. With a paper boarding pass in hand, two hours before wheels up in Doha for LHR, the app still didn't even know I had a second flight to London. The app is an utterly appalling user experience. All the above made for far too tense an experience in both directions, so I won't be using Qatar Airways again. I chose them on the basis of good reviews, and really wanted to like Qatar Airways, but I'm disillusioned. Fairly good in-flight food and good entertainment options are not enough to mitigate all the other issues, which tarnished the start and end of a holiday.
route: London to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

review_text: Our first time in Qatar Airways as a family was our second trip with our toddler & we cannot begin to describe the vast difference in experience we had compared to the other airline. The Qatar Airways crew clearly like their job & honestly the kindest & most helpful flight attendant we have ever encountered is Hristina. Hristina had a smile on her face from the beginning to the end of the trip & she made us feel at home. She was extremely helpful in helping us calm our 18-month-old daughter. In our prior flight with the other airline (when our daughter was only 10 months) they refused to offer us a bassinet even though we had booked for one & no one even bothered checking if our daughter would fit, but not Qatar Airways staff! Hristina made sure to check & even took her supervisors opinion & they ended up allowing our little one to sleep in the bassinet & she had a comfortable sleep. Thank you for the professional & pleasant experience, we look forward to flying with Qatar Airways soon! Flight QR844 on 14 July 2024

The overall flight experience was splendid, stay at Hammad International Airport felt amazing (9 hours layover) without an inconvenience. The flight from Riyadh to Doha was a bit delayed but the connecting flights were properly managed as well as the Pilot notified this when we boarded the plane. Everything was pre-announced to ensure safety of passengers including turbulence indication. Loved how the crew was extremely professional assisting throughout the flight from Doha to Male.

Relatively short notice flights (3 weeks) for a family members funeral. Booked to sit together with extra leg room given we are a tall family. Checked in and received email at 1.30am while all asleep that as the flight was too empty they cancelled it, rebooked us in separate cabins in middle seats on a much later flight, and no extra leg room. Unhelpful staff and help, very reluctant to refund the extra leg room we had already paid for. Flight was cramped with mediocre food and entertainment.

I've flown Qatar now a few times and each time previously I wasn't sure what to write about the airline. I usually fly Emirates from GLA and even though Emirates service has deteriorated in recent years I maintain that they are better. The first 2 sectors from edi to KUL was so uncomfortable. I am only 5 ft and I find the legroom lacking, the width of seat was just fine for me but unfortunately the guy in the middle spilled onto mine and the one on the other side too. Online check-in was unavailable despite being told one can do everything on their app. Customer support via chat was next to useless and telephone calls went unanswered.
route: Edinburgh to Kuala Lumpur via Doha

review_text: Our flight from Nairobi to Doha was three hours late due to mechanical issues causing us to miss our connecting flight to San Francisco. All flights to the United States leave about the same time so we were forced to layover 24 and take the same same flight the next morning. We were met on arrival by an agent and taken to the transfer desk. We were issued tickets for the flight the following day and hotel and food vouchers. We had paid to select bulkhead window seats for our DOH-SFO flight but were assignee bulkhead middle seats instead. We requested a refund and reseating to regular seats but were refused. I also requested compensation for the delay but was also denied. They told us to contact customer service for a refund. We asked that our checked bags be sent to the hotel as they contained all our clothes, toiletries and medicines. After 12 hours we had not received our luggage and I was forced to return to the airport and spend three hours locating it. When I returned home I contacted customer service and once again requested refund of my seat selection charge and compensation for the delay. I was offered $200 non-transferable vouchers for future travel that expired in one year. As we have no travel planned that would go through Doha for the following year these vouchers had no value. I requested cash compensation and was once again denied and the case was closed.
route: Nairobi to San Francisco via Doha

review_text: Flights booked for family Perth/Doha/Dubai. Qatar have moved us to different seats than what was booked. Separate a family from their 6yr old and 9yr old. I've spent 6 hrs being told they can't help. This is regardless of the fact the seats were confirmed. It would appear other passengers are more important than my family who booked well in advance.

The aircraft was in good condition. Boarding were by zone which was strictly followed and very efficient. Food was in a small portion and limited option for Southeast Asians. The cabin crew was doing their job but not the best that I have experienced. The serving of food and beverages was slow on return flights. One young female cabin crew from Indonesia interacted with customers (KUL-DOH sector) and was very hard working.
Amazing airline. Service was excellent and the whole process was made so easy. Food was very delicious. The whole trip was very pleasant and made easy.

review_title: "1 meal service on a 7 hour flight"
review_text: I flew QR a number of times before the pandemic and was looking forward to flying then again. With an airline with a reputation like Qatar Airways, I expect an above average experience compared to other full-service airlines. To my surprise, this time was well below average, both outbound and inbound. Only 1 meal service on a 7 hour flight. The quality of my food was really poor. I ordered the chicken dish and what I received was mashed potatoes with a bland sauce and two minuscule pieces of chicken. Staff was friendly and helpful. So good points there. My impression is that Qatar Airways has been focusing on reducing costs.

Check in, security and automated immigration fast at Dubai terminal 1. Contract lounge decent with a wide range of tasty a la carte dishes. Boarding organized. There was a last minute aircraft change from A350 to 777. Qatar used to inform customer about change of aircraft and therefore change of seats; but that is a thing of the past. Flight uneventful and on time. The long flight departed from a bus gate, which happened to me in over 50% of my 20 arrivals/departures in Doha over the last year. The bus departed only 15 min after I entered, took another 10 min till it reached the aircraft, and stood there for another 15 min because the cleaning crew hasn’t finished. Standing on a bus packed like herrings for 40 min is no fun; especially considering that Hamad airport is suppose to be the best in the world. The flights was conducted on one of the leased CX aircraft, which is always welcomed, because of comfortable seats with a bigger pitch. The entertainment system did not work, but I was given a large IPad instead, which was a nice touch. Food on this flight was ok, and flight was on time. Wifi did not work throughout the flight.

Check in at Bangkok airport efficient and friendly. New Miracle contract lounge at new Bangkok terminal better than those at the old terminal. Boarding well organized. Seats of the aging B777 are poorly padded and have below average seat pitch. Service provided by a flight attendant who really does not enjoy her job. Food poor in quality, taste and presentation. Flight on time. Champagne at the Platinum South lounge at Doha airport has been replaced by not very nice sparkling wine. Departure at Doha airport for Dubai from the feared downstairs Bus gates. It had the atmosphere of a budget terminal with insufficient seating, noise and unorganized boarding. It was off peak time at Doha airport with plenty of airbridges available! Long wait on the bus with open doors at 40 degrees. Short flight to Dubai on time on a wonderful and comfortable A350-1000.
One of the best airlines I ever flew with. The quality of the service and the plane is over the top, and they still managed to be less expensive that other companies! I love them.

Goa (GOX) to Doha (DOH) in Business Class on B787-8. This sector features Qatar's non-Qsuite traditional layout. While not as compelling as Qsuite, it is nevertheless very comfortable. It is a short hop from Goa to Qatar, just under 3 hours, and everything about Qatar Airways was flawless.

Probably one of the first A380s received by Qatar Airways and the age showed. That said the cabin staff were fantastic expressive and attentive, the IFE was superb and the selection was wide though I would suggest a search option. WI-FI wasn’t working for me Qatar Airways need to refurbish their A380s and not wait on Boeing the 777-9s that are a long way off.

Flew Melbourne to Athens via Doha and return July/August 2024. Check in was smooth. Aircraft very clean. Qsuites are amazing. Crew were very professional and friendly, and looked after us extremely well but not intrusive. Once you order your meals these are given to you on your request as it’s meals on demand. Nothing was too much trouble every request was catered to without issue. If you wanted to sleep they would let you sleep, once awake the crew would then offer your meal. Hot towels presented before and after your meal. Toilets were pristine and were cleaned after each person. The PJs were are nice touch on a long haul flight as was the amenity kit. It was one of the best long haul flights and a pleasure to be on board all 4 flights taken. Thank you Qatar Airways we will definitely fly with this company again.

I am dissatisfied with the service from Qatar Airways following my flight on August 2nd. Despite being told that email is the best way to communicate, my emails have been ignored. I only receive responses after submitting new complaints. This shows a lack of commitment.
